title: LakeEnsemblR.WQ Full Workflow
text: vignette provides end--end workflow LakeEnsemblR.WQ: Configure coupled model inputs. Validate model setups. Run model ensembles. Compute harmonized metrics. Prepare run calibration – single-model LHC, optional DE refinement, writing best parameters back, calibrating coupled models (sequentially concurrently) cali_ensemble_wq(). Run sensitivity analysis. Compare visualize model outputs observations.

what: 5.3 Run small LHC test per model
title: LakeEnsemblR.WQ Full Workflow
text: Run one model time. run_lhc_wq() samples parameter space Latin Hypercube Sampling , sample, edits model’s config files model_dir place, runs model, scores . obs_file supplied returns long-format data frame (one row per iteration x observed variable) NSE/RMSE/NRMSE/PBIAS/KGE; otherwise falls back cal_metrics()-derived metrics .
code: cs_glm <- subset(cs_all, model_coupled == "GLM-AED2")  res_glm <- run_lhc_wq(   model          = "GLM-AED2",   param_names    = unique(cs_glm$pars),   calib_setup    = cs_glm,   yaml_file      = "Output.yaml",   model_dir      = "GLM-AED2",   n_samples      = 3,   wq_config_file = "LakeEnsemblR_WQ.yaml",   obs_file       = "obs_data.csv",   # datetime, depth, variable_global_name, value   best_metric    = "KGE",   verbose        = TRUE )  # Note: model_dir's config files are restored to their pre-calibration state # once run_lhc_wq() returns (including on error) -- the function snapshots # model_dir before sampling and copies that snapshot back via on.exit(). Your # working files are not left mutated at the last-tried parameter set.  head(res_glm) attr(res_glm, "best_parameter_set")   # winning parameter set for this model

title: LakeEnsemblR.WQ Full Workflow
text: Set use_de = TRUE run DEoptim LHC phase, seeded best LHC results. DE used, attr(res_glm, "best_parameter_set") overwritten DE’s refined optimum (DE improves LHC seed) – original LHC-phase best still available attr(res_glm, "lhc_best_parameter_set") comparison.
code: res_glm_de <- run_lhc_wq(   model            = "GLM-AED2",   param_names      = unique(cs_glm$pars),   calib_setup      = cs_glm,   yaml_file        = "Output.yaml",   model_dir        = "GLM-AED2",   n_samples        = 20,   wq_config_file   = "LakeEnsemblR_WQ.yaml",   obs_file         = "obs_data.csv",   best_metric      = "KGE",   use_de           = TRUE,   de_iterations    = 30,   de_seed_from_lhc = TRUE,   verbose          = TRUE )  attr(res_glm_de, "best_parameter_set")   # DE-refined best (used for write-back below)

title: LakeEnsemblR.WQ Full Workflow
text: write_best_calib_to_par_files() takes best row (LHC’s, DE’s use_de = TRUE) writes module par_file CSVs referenced LakeEnsemblR_WQ.yaml (directly model config files write_target = "config"). Re-run export_config_wq() afterward need values pushed way model folders final verification run.
code: write_best_calib_to_par_files(   lhc_results  = res_glm_de,   calib_setup  = cs_glm,   config_file  = "LakeEnsemblR_WQ.yaml",   folder       = ".",   metric       = "KGE",   write_target = "par_file" )

what: 5.6 Calibrate all coupled models with cali_ensemble_wq()
title: LakeEnsemblR.WQ Full Workflow
text: cali_ensemble_wq() multi-model wrapper around run_lhc_wq(): pass full models vector combined cs_all table (split automatically model_coupled) runs, scores, optionally writes back best parameters every model one call. default four models run sequentially. Since model model_dir, can safely run concurrently instead, one worker process per model: parallel_models stacks parallel/de_parallel (parallelize LHC samples DE evaluations within one model’s run) – turn multiple together, size worker counts n_model_workers * n_workers (* de_n_workers) doesn’t oversubscribe CPU cores. Worker processes attached console, parallel_models = TRUE progress messages written <output_dir>/cali_ensemble_wq_workers.log instead printing live – tail file watch progress runs.
code: models_coupled <- c("GLM-AED2", "GOTM-WET", "GOTM-Selmaprotbas", "Simstrat-AED2")  result <- cali_ensemble_wq(   models         = models_coupled,   calib_setup    = cs_all,   yaml_file      = "Output.yaml",   folder         = ".",   n_samples      = 20,   obs_file       = "obs_data.csv",   wq_config_file = "LakeEnsemblR_WQ.yaml",   best_metric    = "KGE",   use_de         = TRUE,   de_iterations  = 30,   verbose        = TRUE,   on_error       = "skip",       # a failing model doesn't stop the others   save_results   = TRUE,   output_dir     = "calibration_results",   write_best     = TRUE,   write_target   = "par_file",   config_file    = "LakeEnsemblR_WQ.yaml" )  result$summary                              # one row per model: success, n_rows, n_stats result$best_parameter_sets[["GLM-AED2"]]     # winning parameters for one model result$write_back[["GLM-AED2"]]              # whether the write-back succeeded result_parallel <- cali_ensemble_wq(   models          = models_coupled,   calib_setup     = cs_all,   yaml_file       = "Output.yaml",   folder          = ".",   n_samples       = 20,   obs_file        = "obs_data.csv",   wq_config_file  = "LakeEnsemblR_WQ.yaml",   best_metric     = "KGE",   parallel_models = TRUE,   # run all requested models at the same time   n_model_workers = 4,      # one worker process per model   verbose         = TRUE,   save_results    = TRUE,   output_dir      = "calibration_results",   write_best      = TRUE,   config_file     = "LakeEnsemblR_WQ.yaml" )

title: LakeEnsemblR.WQ
text: Run, harmonize, compare water quality simulations across multiple 1D lake model frameworks. LakeEnsemblR.WQ extends LakeEnsemblR workflows tools : configure model-specific WQ settings, validate model setups run, run model ensembles, calibrate model ensembles – Latin Hypercube sampling, optional Differential Evolution refinement, single-model -coupled-models--(sequential concurrent) calibration via cali_ensemble_wq(), extract harmonize output variables, calculate standardized ecosystem metrics summary statistics, compare visualize model outputs observations. Supported model frameworks: GLM-AED2 GOTM-WET GOTM-Selmaprotbas Simstrat-AED2

title: LakeEnsemblR.WQ
text: Several dependencies GitHub-wrappers around external lake model binaries (GLM3r, WETr, SelmaprotbasR, SimstratR, MyLakeR) optional – need ones matching model(s) actually run (run_ensemble_wq()/run_lhc_wq() check call time give informative error missing). ’re declared Remotes: DESCRIPTION remotes::install_github() resolves automatically. glmtools (hard dependency, needed regardless model run) known upstream packaging gap: DESCRIPTION depends GLM3r doesn’t declare find , can make dependency-solving tools (pak, extension devtools::install_deps()/build_vignettes()) fail find package called GLM3r even though GLM3r package’s Remotes:. hit , pre-install GLM3r first:
code: # install.packages("remotes") # Currently in development on tubabucak's fork; will move to the aemon-j # organization (https://github.com/aemon-j/LakeEnsemblR.WQ) after release. remotes::install_github("tubabucak/LakeEnsemblR.WQ") remotes::install_github("aemon-j/GLM3r@v3.3") remotes::install_github("tubabucak/LakeEnsemblR.WQ")

code: library(LakeEnsemblR)  # 0) Export model-specific configuration and inputs for physical setup  export_config(   config_file = "LakeEnsemblR.yaml",   folder = ".",   verbose = TRUE )  library(LakeEnsemblR.WQ)   # 1) Export model-specific configuration and inputs for WQ setup export_config_wq(   config_file = "LakeEnsemblR_WQ.yaml",   folder = ".",   verbose = TRUE )  # 2) Run selected models run_res <- run_ensemble_wq(   config_file = "LakeEnsemblR_WQ.yaml",   models = c("GLM-AED2", "GOTM-WET", "GOTM-Selmaprotbas", "SIMSTRAT-AED2"),   folder = ".",   validate = TRUE,   verbose = TRUE )  # 3) Compute metrics listed in Output.yaml metric_out <- cal_metrics(   metric_yaml_file = "Output.yaml",   model_filter = "all",   wq_config_file = "LakeEnsemblR_WQ.yaml" )  # 4) Optional: compute summary statistics stat_out <- cal_stats(metric_out)

title: Calibrate an ensemble of LakeEnsemblR.WQ models — cali_ensemble_wq
text: models Character vector models calibrate. Supported values "GLM-AED2", "GOTM-WET", "GOTM-Selmaprotbas", "Simstrat-AED2". Matching case-insensitive. calib_setup Either data frame calib_setup format named list data frames. data frame supplied contains model_coupled, rows split model. yaml_file Character scalar, vector, named list. Path(s) metric YAML file(s) passed run_lhc_wq(). folder Character. Base folder used resolve relative paths optional write-back operations. model_dirs Character vector/list model directories. Can scalar (reused), length length(models) (positional), named model. Defaults file.path(folder, models). param_names Optional. Either character vector reused models, named list/vector model. NULL, uses unique(calib_setup$pars) per model. model_filter Optional model filter(s) passed run_lhc_wq(). Accepts scalar, length-length(models) vector, named vector/list. wq_config_file Character scalar, vector, named list WQ config path(s) passed run_lhc_wq(). n_samples Integer. Number LHS samples per model. yaml_file_model Character scalar/vector/list. GOTM YAML file name(s) passed run_lhc_wq(). par_file Character scalar/vector/list. Simstrat .par file name(s) passed run_lhc_wq(). obs_file Optional observed-data CSV path. supplied, model returns flattened stats data.frame run_lhc_wq(). obs_to_model_units Logical. Passed run_lhc_wq(). spin_up_days Numeric NULL. Passed run_lhc_wq(). stats_by_depth Logical. Passed run_lhc_wq(). return_best Logical. Passed run_lhc_wq(). best_metric Character. Passed run_lhc_wq(). parallel Logical. Passed run_lhc_wq() (parallelizes LHC samples within single model's run). n_workers Integer NULL. Passed run_lhc_wq(). parallel_dir Character. Passed run_lhc_wq(). keep_worker_dirs Logical. Passed run_lhc_wq(). use_de Logical. Passed run_lhc_wq(). Scalar, length length(models), named model. de_iterations Integer. Passed run_lhc_wq(). de_popsize Integer NULL. Passed run_lhc_wq(). de_f Numeric. Passed run_lhc_wq(). de_cr Numeric. Passed run_lhc_wq(). de_seed_from_lhc Logical. Passed run_lhc_wq(). de_parallel Logical. Passed run_lhc_wq() (parallelizes DE evaluations within single model's run). de_n_workers Integer NULL. Passed run_lhc_wq(). parallel_models Logical. TRUE, calibrate requested models concurrently (one worker process per model) instead sequentially. model model_dir, running concurrently risk -place file collisions apply parallel/de_parallel workers sharing single model_dir. NOTE: stacks parallel/de_parallel – also enabled, total worker processes can multiply (n_model_workers * n_workers n_model_workers * de_n_workers). Size worker counts stay within available CPU cores. Default FALSE (sequential, matching prior behavior). n_model_workers Integer NULL. Number concurrent worker processes used parallel_models = TRUE. Defaults min(length(models), detectCores(logical = FALSE) - 1). verbose Logical. Print progress messages. on_error Character. One "skip" "stop". save_results Logical. TRUE, save model result RDS. output_dir Character. Directory saved RDS files save_results = TRUE. Defaults folder. output_prefix Character. Prefix saved result files save_results = TRUE. write_best Logical. TRUE, call write_best_calib_to_par_files() successful model. write_target Character. Passed write_best_calib_to_par_files(). config_file Character. Required write_best = TRUE; path WQ master config used write_best_calib_to_par_files().

code: write_best_calib_to_par_files(   lhc_results,   calib_setup,   config_file,   folder = ".",   metric = "KGE",   minimize = FALSE,   write_target = c("par_file", "config"),   verbose = TRUE )

title: Write best calibration parameter set back to par_file CSVs — write_best_calib_to_par_files
text: lhc_results data.frame; output run_lhc_wq() obs_file supplied.  Must contain one column per calibrated parameter least one performance-metric column (e.g. NSE, KGE). calib_setup data.frame; calibration setup table used running run_lhc_wq(), produced calib_setup_from_tables. Must contain columns pars, model_coupled, domain, process, subprocess, optionally group_name. config_file character; name LakeEnsemblR_WQ.yaml master config file. folder character; path directory containing config_file. Defaults ".". metric character; name performance metric column lhc_results use selecting best run. Defaults "KGE".  metric column name may prefixed variable name (e.g. "oxygen_NSE"); function looks exact match first, falls back columns contain metric. minimize logical; TRUE row lowest metric value chosen (e.g. RMSE).  FALSE (default) row highest value chosen (e.g. KGE, NSE). write_target character; write best values. One "par_file" (default, updates module par_file CSVs) "config" (uses set_value_config() write directly model config files). verbose logical; TRUE print message parameter updated.  Defaults TRUE.

title: Write best calibration parameter set back to par_file CSVs — write_best_calib_to_par_files
